metadata.name: usd_fra_3x6
metadata.description: Bloomberg SWPM USD 2M x 5M FRA captured from screens on 2026-05-02.
metadata.source_detail: Bloomberg SWPM FRA, curve 490 USD SOFR discounting, curve 559 USD 3M CME Term SOFR projection, curve date 2026-05-02, valuation date 2026-05-05 Phase-2 envelope migration (2026-05-09): hybrid Tier-AB. The USD-SOFR discount curve is now calibrated from 12 SWPM Curve 490 input rates (deposits + OIS swaps) using the per-step ois_compounding override (CompoundedWithRateCutoff{cutoff_days: 1}). The USD-3M-CME-TERM-SOFR forward curve is a synthetic flat 3.6437% test curve that doesn't correspond to a Bloomberg-conformant calibration; it is preserved verbatim in initial_market.curves.
metadata.regen_command: manual Bloomberg SWPM screen transcription; see provenance.screenshots

screenshots.description: Main SWPM FRA terms and valuation results for the USD 2M x 5M sell/lend FRA
Bloomberg cashflow table with reset rate, settlement cashflow, discount factor, and NPV
Curve 490 USD SOFR discount curve screen with visible market rates, zero rates, and discount factors
Curve 559 USD 3M CME Term SOFR projection curve screen with visible zero rates and discount factors

plan.description: Hybrid Tier-AB migration: USD-SOFR discount curve calibrated from Bloomberg SWPM Curve 490 Market Rates (12 quotes: 1W-3W deposits, 1M-2Y OIS swaps) transcribed from `bloomberg_reference.discount_curve_screen`. OIS compounding override `CompoundedWithRateCutoff{cutoff_days: 1}` matches Bloomberg's SWPM convention. The forward curve (USD-3M-CME-TERM-SOFR) is a flat 3.6437% test curve engineered to make this specific FRA at-the-money — not a faithful Bloomberg curve — so it is preserved in initial_market.curves rather than calibrated.

npv.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg cashflow and NPV are displayed to cents; the screen shows -0.01 while the exact par FRA economics round to zero. abs-or-rel: absolute/display tolerance is the intended comparison gate; relative tolerance is retained only as a legacy diagnostic.

pv01.tolerance_reason: Bloomberg BR01/DV01 displayed to cents; Phase-2 envelope migration (2026-05-09) replaces the materialized USD-SOFR discount snapshot with a calibrated curve (12 SWPM Curve 490 quotes + ois_compounding override = CompoundedWithRateCutoff{cutoff_days: 1}). The calibrated curve differs from Bloomberg's snapshot DFs by ~4e-4 at the FRA's pay_date due to schedule/day-count nuances inside Bloomberg's pricing kernel (see docs/archive/plans/2026-05-09-golden-fixture-envelope-migration-plan.md §14). The forward curve (USD-3M-CME-TERM-SOFR) is preserved verbatim in initial_market.curves, so par_rate and NPV remain bit-stable; only sensitivities — which combine forward-rate shocks with discount-curve values — show the calibration drift.
